description:
CyberTank! is a set of rules for an arena combat game involving futuristic tanks. (The players provide their own hex map, counters, and dice.) Tanks have elaborate designs, with many different possible types of weapon, different firing arcs for the weapons, drones, armor, ECM and ECCM, wheeled vs. tracked movement, etc. A number of sample designs are provided and players are encouraged to create their own. The game system is fairly simple, with most of the complexity coming from the interaction of the various systems.

The game was made available at a 1992 convention as a "pre-edition beta." It is not certain if there were any further versions of the game.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A free Print and Play game ruthlessly taking the deck building mechanism from Dominion, Ascension, Resident Evil etc. However instead of a bleak and bleary fantasy world, players take the roles of football (soccer) team managers and build their club up to challenge for league glory!

Buy in squad players, build stadium improvements, get yourself a feeder team, hire physios, coaches and an assistant manager as you develop your squad to become the best it can be and take on the best teams in the league!

Managers start with a small deck of cards consisting of a few average players and some money. On their turn a manager can use the money to buy new items from a selection available and they can turn to their players to play games against a dozen different teams, some stronger than others. Over the course of the game, the manager's club (their hand of cards) will grow bigger and stronger, allowing them to compete against and beat the stronger opposition teams in the game. Build For The Title is all about building a strong and streamlined club that is capable of beating everyone in their way.

boardgame: Blood, Steel and Sand: The Battle of First Alamein
description:
Blood, Steel & Sand takes the Rohrbaugh's "quick-play" Blood and Steel series wargame to the deserts of North Africa. Like Blood and Steel, the game's complexity is low with only minimal additional rules. The unit sizes are Regiments & Brigades.

The players assume the commands of Rommel and Auchinlek.  The game uses the innovative chartless CRT and the chit activation system to allow players to maneuver and fight their formations.

Components

     11x17" map
     168 single-sided, unmounted counters
     8 pages of rules and charts

StrikerZ is a light and fast soccer game, with the bottom of the box serving as the playing arena.

Each player has a "team" of five dice bearing the traditional numbers from 2 to 11, with one #14 substitute. (The #1 keeper is on the shooting die.) During a turn, both players roll their dice in the arena, with the player who rolls the higher sum getting the chance to shoot for a goal by rolling the green die – which will result in a goal, stop or corner kick.

If your dice total is much less than the opponent, you receive a yellow card. Rolling a manager icon lets you change the result of another die; the dice also have whistle and substitution icons.

The first player to score five goals wins.

From the website:

Épica is a collectible card game where you embody a powerful hero of the Shadowlands, wielding a Celestial Weapon that allows you to run wild in battle, pushing your abilities to the limit.

Your mission is to protect the supremacy of your Clan, defeating the rival heroes. But Épica is not only about battling, it's a unique gaming experience, an authentic author's game, perfectly worked and developed for over 2 years.

In "Épica: Dark Ages", the first installment of the game, you'll find a booklet describing the game rules and an innovative History Mode, where you'll be submerged into an adventure story playable by one or more players, telling of the outcome of the battles that happen across the Shadowlands during these tragic times.

There are two additional types of game: Battle Mode, where a single deck makes possible to engage up to 10 players, each embodying a different hero of this shadowy world. And, of course, Tournament Mode, where you can combine many sets of the game and upcoming expansions to build your own playing deck and participate in the many events and contests that will be arranged across all Spain.

In the box, you'll also find more than 100 finely illustrated cards that will amaze you for their quality and realism. Think of each card as a little gem, fully tested and developed.

War has begun, the Oni hybrid warriors, crossing of demons and humans, are willing to conquer the Shadowlands. Only a few humans stand before their ruthless advance. Will you be able to stop them?


De su página web:

Épica es un juego de cartas coleccionable, en el cuál encarnas a un poderoso héroe de las Tierras de las Sombras capaz de blandir un arma celestial que le permite desatarse en batalla. Llevando sus habilidades más allá del límite.

Tu misión fundamental es proteger la supremacía de tu clan derrotando a los héroes rivales. Pero Épica no es sólo lucha, es algo más, es una experiencia única de juego, perfectamente trabajado y desarrollado durante más de 2 años, un auténtico juego de autor.

Dentro de Épica, Edades Oscuras, encontrarás un libro que te explicará las reglas del juego, así como un novedoso modo historia, el cual te sumergirá en una auténtica novela auto jugable por uno o varios jugadores, en ella se narra el desenlace de las batallas que ocurren en las Tierras de la Sombra durante esta terrible época.

Existen otros 2 tipos de juego adicionales: el modo Batalla, en el cual con un solo mazo podrás realizar combates de hasta 10 jugadores encarnando a uno de los héroes de este sombrío mundo. Y por supuesto, el modo Torneo, en el cuál combinando varios sets de juego y las sucesivas ampliaciones, podrás crearte tu propio mazo de juego temático con el cuál podrás competir en las distintas competiciones y eventos que se realicen por toda la geografía.

También en el interior de la caja, encontrarás más de 100 cartas excelentemente ilustradas que te sorprenderán por su realismo y calidad. Cada una de ellas es como una pequeña joya desarrollada y testeada al más mínimo detalle.

La guerra ha comenzado, los Oni guerreros híbridos nacidos del cruce entre humanos y demonios, están dispuestos a conquistar las Tierras de la Sombra. Sólo una pequeña defensa humana podrá detener su imparable avance. ¿ Podrás detenerlos ?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – Into Tartarus Asylum Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Into Tartarus explores the kernel of truth at the root of every myth with 60 new cards (three copies each of 20 unique cards) for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game. The fifth Asylum Pack in the "Ancient Relics" cycle, Into Tartarus transports archaeologists, investigators, cultists, and criminals to Greece where each faction uncovers new chaos while searching ancient ruins for powerful relics.

Several of the cards from Into Tartarus enable players to stir up all new manners of chaos. Agency decks gain cards to work against the chaos, while Cthulhu and Shub-Niggurath decks gain terrifying new attachments. Several factions gain powerful new Relics, further expanding deck-building options centered around these strange and powerful items.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Call of Cthulhu – The Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Dobbelduel (Dice Duel) is a code-breaking game similar to Mastermind.

At the start of the game, one of the players creates a random code by rolling four dice. The other player has to guess this code. He gets seven rounds to gather information. At the start of each round, he rolls four dice, then uses some or all of them to create a (partial) code. He then receives feedback on this code, being told how many dice are equal to the corresponding die in the code, how many are higher and how many are lower. When he thinks he has guessed the code, or after he has used up his seven rounds, he makes a guess. If this guess is correct, he scores a number of points, depending on the amount of rounds and dice used.

After this, the rolls are reveresed, and the other player has to break a code. The player with the highest score wins.

Grabbelen (Grabble) is a party game based on memory and card recognition.

On the table are 36 cards, and each card has a color (and symbol matching that color) and a number (1 through 6). A player rolls two dice – one with numbers, the other with colors – then all players look for the card that matches the color and number rolled. The first player to find the card takes it.

If the card isn't on the table, the first player to call out the name of the player who has it takes the card from its current owner.

The first player to accumulate a set number of cards wins.

"Little Mouse" is a children's sequencing game which uses the imagery and phrasing from the book, "The Little Mouse, the Red Ripe Strawberry, and the Big Hungry Bear" by Audrey and Don Wood.  The game contains 48 thick cards with 4 each of the 12 different phrases to play any of six different games.

[paraphrased rules]

1. Solo Sequencing
Solitary sequencing in which one set of 12 different cards are laid face-up.  The player places the cards in sequence according to the original story.

2. Scramble Sequencing
For 2-4 players.  All 48 cards are placed face-up.  The first player to complete a sequence wins.

3. Skill Sequencing
For 2-4 players. Cards are shuffled.  The top four cards are laid face-up, the rest are dealt to the players.  Each turn, the player places a card before or after one of the four middle cards.  First player to use all cards wins.

4. Quartet
For 2-4 players.  Players seek to obtain all four of a particular card.  Player with the highest number of sets wins.

5. Snap
For 2-4 players.  Traditional Snap game.

6. Matching Words to Pictures
For 2-4 players. A single storyline set is dealt to each player.  A leader displays either picture or word card and the other players have to match its opposite (words-to-picture or picture-to-words)

Game description from the publisher:

The Russian soldati quaked as the hulking blue figure approached. One panicked, dropping his rifle, and fled. He was the lucky man, the only survivor. The remaining Russians expected the giant to fight like a brute, especially after their bullets bounced off him with no effect, but instead of running at the men, he stopped short and glared with such unearthly ferocity, he bent the commander's mind.

Drawn forth from Naraka by a blend of science and mysticism, Itami is a hybrid of human and Oni, the denizens of the Realm of Suffering. While even the Shōgunate do not fully understand his abilities, they are astounding. His gaze can wrack others with pain and bend their very will. An unholy penumbra seeps out his every pore and drains the essences of those around him.

For now, Itami works to ensure the mutual benefits of the Shōgunate and the Inferior Worlds, swatting aside enemies with a kenabō wrapped in mystically imbued leather. The Shōgunate have placed him in Iroh Minamoto's 3rd Division to best make use of his abilities while they explore their tenuous alliance with the Reich.

Itami includes new rules for the Nippon Accord and a unique scenario that highlights Itami's mysterious powers.

The Sideshow comes complete with three promotional products - expansion cards, collector's dice, and the game Lucky Dice. These products will never be offered for sale in stores by Dice Hate Me Games.

This six-card addition to your Carnival box is sure to add even more fun! These cards replace the Wilds from the base game and all feature a character from the carnival sideshow. In play, they can behave just as the original Wilds or they can be discarded to perform the event specified on the card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Slim to None: The Campaign Along the Slim River, January 1942
description:
“Heedless of danger and of their isolation they had shattered the division: they had captured the Slim Bridge by their reckless and gallant determination.”

--Lt. Colonel Arthur Anderson, 155th Battery.

Slim to None is a war game simulation of the battle along the Slim River in northern Malaya.  In January 1942, the 11th Indian Division was sent north to contest the Imperial Japanese Army's advance from their landing beaches on the Kra Isthmus.

However, the Japanese 5th Infantry Division, supported by the 4th Armored Regiment struck first in the early morning hours of January 7th at the Cluny Rubber Plantation and at the town of Trolak, just north of the Slim River. The Allied forces were caught unprepared by the sudden Japanese combined arms offensive, with units scattered along 19 miles of road north of the Slim River, and with little to no weaponry by which to oppose the Japanese armor. Worse still, the Japanese infiltration tactics through jungle terrain the Allied commanders believed to be impenetrable, led to the Allied battalions being repeatedly outflanked and forced to retreat.

The Japanese success here would eventually take them all the way to Singapore. Can you do better?

GAME COMPONENTS

     7 page rules booklet
     One 11" by 17" map
     165 single-sided, un-mounted counters


The game can be played solitaire as well as with an opponent. The game is part of the Blood and Steel series of games from High Flying Dice Games.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: British Paratroops, 3rd Para Brigade – "Red Devils"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Axis soldiers had pinned down Captain Joseph Brown where they had bunkered in a Scapa Flow alley. As he and his men watched bullets trace the air over their heads, the thundering footsteps of a combat walker began to approach. Before he even saw the walker, he heard its massive cannon blast, and a nearby wall exploded, sending rubble flying. "Where are those reinforcements?" the Captain shouted. He didn't hear his sergeant's response, but saw, instead, the sun glinting off three heavily armored paratroopers.

Save the day with the Allied Red Devils, the elite armored members of the British 3rd Paratroop Brigade. Land them on the battlefield to launch powerful energy pulses at enemy combat walkers with their 60W Phaser Guns. Where other infantry weapons may fail to punch the reinforced armor of combat walkers, the Red Devils have the firepower to do the job. Introduce their phaser weapons and their Rocket Punches to the battlefield. Shrug off small munitions.

Turn the tide of battle with these elite paratroopers. Your Dust Tactics troops will know when these reinforcements have arrived!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Schwer Sturmgrenadiere Ausf. A – "Heavy Recon Grenadiers"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Allied forces sought shelter in an abandoned warehouse. The communications officer's voice was drowned out by the sounds of wood shattering and the rapport of heavy machine guns. Three Axis soldiers marched heavily into the warehouse. Because of the darkness and obstructing rows of crates, it was hard to tell, but they appeared to be wearing battle suits of heavy armor.

Engage and destroy Allied squads with the Axis Heavy Recon Grenadiers, an armored squad expansion for Dust Tactics. Marching onto battlefields in combat armor, these soldiers can shrug off enemy gunfire and are practically invulnerable to infantry small arms fire. Their enemies are much less fortunate. Each member of the Heavy Recon Grenadiers wields two powerful MG 44 rifles designed to mow down Allied infantry in a lead rain. Take command of these elite soldiers, 
shrug off enemy fire, and turn the tide of the war!

Three unpainted miniatures are exquisitely sculpted in dynamic battle-ready poses. The Heavy Recon Grenadiers make deadly squad killers, and their resilient body armor lets them join with some armored heroes.

boardgame: High Seas Fleet: The First World War at Sea
description:
Subtitle: "Data Annexes: Ships & Aircraft August 1914 - November 1918".

(from Clash of Arms:)

This annex booklet provides the statistics for all the ships, aricraft, and weapons used in the scearios in High Seas Fleet.

We've also tried to cover most of the other major and minor naval weapons used in the Great War, so that the players will be able to create their own scenarios using the information here.  
In addition to the data tables in Annexes A through F, Annex J lists all the formulas used to calculate a ships damaged points, armor rating, and other game-related values. Players can use this Annex to add other ships to the system.

description:
Square Up is a pattern matching game using a 2x3 portion of a 3x3 gameboard. Four players are shown on box top, but it may be possible that as few as two could play.

The players are dealt cards intended to match patterns on the board. The colors in the 3x3 matrix could be changed one at a time by rotation of an associated knob on the board. Assumable a player would rotate one of the knobs to his color on his turn.

From developer's site:

Want to pursue your dreams at an art school? Want to be a campus sensation at a big state school? Want to work crazy hard for that full ride to a very selective private university? The choice is yours, and the path you take there is entirely up to you.

Application Crunch is a card game for 3-4 players. Each player takes on the role of a high school student and college applicant. Players juggle their time between academics, extracurricular activities, work, and service while competing for college applications and scholarships.

To win, players must get into college, save enough money to pay for tuition and other expenses, and build up the kind of character who can succeed in their chosen school.

description: The Game of Pegpen is a sort of Dot and Boxes game on the 5x5 grid with a significant twist. Normally in Dots and Boxes a square is enclosed with move marks. However in The Game Of Pegpen  requires the players to peg each corner of the square as well as the middle position of each line on the square. Thus 8 moves are required to close the box.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – On Dangerous Grounds
description:
Game description from the publisher:

On Dangerous Grounds is the third monthly installment of 60 fixed cards (three copies each of 20 different cards) for the "A Tale of Champions" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. The cards in this Chapter Pack allow players to augment existing decks or create entirely new decks as you respond to challenges in exciting new ways.

On Dangerous Grounds expands and develops the focus on Melee running throughout the "A Tale of Champions" cycle as new cards encourage players to forge and break alliances. Ladies attend the tourney grounds, and as the contestants struggle for supremacy, many of the cards from On Dangerous Grounds reward players who win early challenges.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description: Promotional game, made for the milk delivery firm, Unigate Dairies. Business game of buying and selling groceries.  A four stage game.  1) puchasing products, 2) marketing expenditure, 3) selling products, 4) marketing income.  An unusual mechanism gives the players a variety of choices of what to buy and sell and at what prices. The marketing phases involve moving around various tracks on the board, with options on where to move next, and choosing how to make the best of the opportunities presented.

BANG! Gold Rush is an expansion for the card game BANG! that can be used with the base game alone or mixed with other expansions. In addition to featuring eight new characters, BANG! Gold Rush adds two new mechanisms to game play:

• Players can collect gold nuggets to buy equipment, with the equipment giving your character new abilities and you the chance to develop new strategies.

• Even when a player is shot down during the game, he can come back to play with the Shadow Gunslinger variant. When that dead player's turn comes around, he temporarily re-enters play and takes his turn as if he were alive, now giving an eliminated player the chance to affect the outcome of a game. Look out for shots from beyond the grave!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Panic Station: Survival Kit Mini Expansion
description:
This mini-expansion contains 6 cards and can be considered a "Survival Kit" for the Humans in the game.

The cards in "Panic Station: Survival Kit mini-expansion" are:

2 Night Goggles:  This allows players to search an already searched room (one time use) without a Parasite spawning.

2 Parasite Signals:  This allows a player to lure a Parasite away from a location where it would otherwise land.  This would normally be used to ensure that Parasites do not land in a space with Humans.  Of course, if this item is in the hands of the Host or an infected player, it can be used a little more devlishly.

1 Sniper Rifle:  This ranged weapon allows players to target Parasites and Players up to 2 rooms away, and thus it is a very powerful weapon to defend yourself or attack another without the need to move to a more dangerous location.

1 Antidote: this is a one-time use injection. If this card is traded to an infected player, the player will be cured of the infection and be turned back to Human.  This can be very dramatic, since it can give a human team on the brink of defeat a way to fight back.

boardgame: Black Powder: The Last Argument of Kings
description:
At the beginning of the Eighteenth century, warfare entered a new era. The widespread use of the flintlock musket had transformed the way armies trained and fought.
This period was a golden age for the crowned heads of Europe. They commanded professional troops, gloriously outfitted in splendid uniforms, and fought each other over land, wealth and the succession of their thrones. Some even led their armies on the field of battle. Yet by the end of the century, the real power of kings was gone or greatly eroded parliaments would decide the foreing policy of nations, democracy had taken hold in the New World and the divine right of kings was being questioned everywhere.
These were the last wars fought to protect the succession rights of royalty. These wars were the Last Arguments of Kings.
This supplement for the Black Powder game overviews the main conflicts and armies of this period, and includes army lists and special rules to enable you to refight these wars using the Black Powder rules, as well as scenarios for the most dramatic battles of the time. Featured conflicts include:
The War of the Spanish Succession (The Battle of Blenheim 1704)
The Great Northen War (The Battle of Holowczyn 1708)
The Austro-Turkish Wars (The Battle of Petrovardin 1716)
The War of the Austrian Succession (The Battle of Fontenoy 1745)
The Wars of the English Succession (The '45 Rebellion)
The Seven Years' War (The Battle of Hundorf 1762)
War in the Colonies (The French Indian Wars & The War in India)
Raids and Invasions (Amphibious Warfare in the 18th Century)
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession
History: War of the Spanish Succession

description:
Airlines Europe: Flugverbot – a free expansion distributed at Spiel 2011 – provides players a blocking variant for Alan R. Moon's 2011 release Airlines Europe that, in the words of Abacusspiele's Matthias Wagner, "should make the board/routes slightly tighter".

Airlines Europe: Flugverbot introduces a revised version of the sabotage rules present in Moon's 1990 release Airlines in which players can block locations on possible airline routes. ("Flugverbot" = "flight ban")

Help Bob and his friends to build a tower! How can you do it? Easy: With the coloured die and a little luck! Because of it's simple set of rules, the game is suitable for kids from 3 years up and is even fun playing it alone!

The goal of the game is to move Bob onward from field to field and to build the highest tower.

When it is your turn, you roll the coloured die and when you roll the colour of the next field, or you roll the Bob face, you move Bob to the next field and get one building block for your tower. The game ends, when all tower pieces are used up.

Very simple die rolling game for young kids.

Like the Star Trek Starship Tactical Combat Simulator and its other book form expansions, the Ship Construction Manual is called a supplement for the Fasa Star Trek Role Playing Game but primarily has material for the Combat Simulator boardgame.  This supplement includes everything a player needs to generate all new starships, as well as all of the necessary statistics for the use in the Combat Simulator board game. In addition, there is background material usefull for the role playing game and some basic guidelines on creating deck plans based on your ship designs.

From the cover:

Begin your career as a starship engineer.

The Ship Construction Manual contains all the information necessary to construct starships for your STAR TREK Role Playing Game.  The recently declassified information in this manual allows the design of state-of-the-art starships of Federation, Klingon, Romulan, Gorn, or Orion origin. The easy-to-use format combined with the comprehensive data also provides the information necessary to build any ship active during the last 60 years of Star Trek history.

The 80-page rulebook includes the tables for the various engines, weapons, shields, and computers that make up starships, essays on the design philosophy of the major races, detailed information on the cost and availability of the equipment, and a system for rating the ships in combat.

The Ship Construction Manual is required reading for all Star Fleet personnel and potential ship designers from all of the major races.

Stack Flip Crush Evict is a game that is played with a regular set of 24 checkers (two sets 12 checkers, each set a different color), and no board.  Players are challenged to make 3 moves during their turn that consist of doing one of the following actions: stacking checkers on other checkers (or on the play are), flipping a stack, crushing a stack of opponent's checkers between a player's own pieces, or removing one of their free pieces from the board.

During play, the object is to either get 3 of their checkers in a stack together to be removed 4 times, or force their opponent to not have a legal move.

Included in the game is basic rules for play on a single stack

Scrapok is a card game which mixes the spirit of competition of a game of bet such as the famous Poker with the reflection of a game of words. 
The aim of the game is to take the chips of other players having the longest word with private and common cards or at least to make the other players believing it. The cards are discovered in several step where gamer must bet, bluff, raise, fold.
Scrapok is played with chips and special cards with letters and 4 signs   Heart,   Diamonds,   Spades,   Clubs

At the beginning, each player recive 5 cards. At the end of the first round of betting three cards are turned over and seen by all players, then, in each of the following two rounds of betting, another card is emerging. At each round of betting and depending on their games, players fold, call, bet, raise, check as poker.
At the end, players not fold must form the longest word. In case of equality, color cards and signs make up the tie.

From the publisher:

Lords are gathering warriors to their sides to defeat their foes, claim powerful artifacts and grow rich in gold. This is a miniatures battle game where the Lord and his warriors are on the field to sow terror and reap glory. All melodrama aside, DTB is a quick and simple battle game. Any kind of miniature can be used as long as it is of a similar scale as the others used. Figures are not even needed. Slips of paper or different color poker chips can be used. As long as all players know who belongs to who, it does not matter. Players keep their record sheets to bring their Lords and warriors back to the table to further increase their spoils.

description:
Statistical replay games on Athletic events, featuring in the basic set all the inside the lanes racing events from Munich 1972, both men and women competitions.
Each runner in each competition is rated for a Class of Ranking, from A to E, corresponding to one of three resolution Tables for the various distances of the competitions.
For each single race is given an average run time, valid for all competitors, then you have to roll a couple of dice for each section of the race (from 2 rolls in case of a 100 meters event, up to 8 rolls for 400 meters races) to see how the runner gains or loses time in respect to that average.
There are also rules for false starts and other infractions that can lead to disqualification, rules for variable form of the competitors and for the capacity to win a frantic affair.

Several expansion sets for other Olympic Games and World Championships will be released later
Lancaster: The New Laws introduces 18 new laws for the Lancaster board game on which the players can vote. These law cards can replace those in the original game or be mixed with them.

A famous globetrotter has made a secret journey and the players try to find out which famous sites around the world it included.  This is done by removing some cards from a deck at the start and hiding them.  The rest are distributed, and as a player reaches one of their destination cards they reveal it, but also get to see one of an opponent's cards as well.  The idea being that once you have seen all the cards except those hidden you will know the secret destinations and win. Attractive large board showing available routes.

boardgame: Bloody Hell: Operations Goodwood and Spring
description:
When I came to the northern edge of the village (Cagny), I saw to my dismay about 25 or 30 British tanks which had already passed southward over the main road to Caen which ran from east to west.  The whole area was dotted with British tanks which were slowly rolling south against no opposition. How could I plug the gap?

--Colonel Hans von Luck, 21st Panzer Division on July 18th when Operation Goodwood began.

INTRODUCTION

Historians have described the British attack near Caen and the Canadian assault against Verrieres Ridge as disasters.  Indeed, following these operations the Germans were able to transfer to the western sector of Normandy much of their surviving armor to deal with the American offensive. Nonetheless, recently discovered material and some oral histories have shown the Allied offensives came close to success. Can you do as well, or better?

Bloody Hell is designed as a "two-games-in-one" package allowing the players to play either of General Montgomery's operations (securing Caen and then breaking out to support Patton's Operation Cobra). Players can also combine both games into a grand-tactical simulation pitting Montgomery against General Sepp Dietrich and the 1st SS Panzer Corps.

Game Components

Each copy of Bloody Hell is composed of the following:


     Four “11” by “17” map sections
     280 double-sided, unmounted counters
     Two sheets of game tables and charts
     One set of rules

boardgame: Tough Hombres: The Battle of Mairy
description:
Another game in the very popular and easy to play Blood and Steel series. Tough Hombres is the third game on famous battles fought during the 1944 Lorraine Campaign (Patton's Finest and Revanche: The Battle of Dompaire are the other two).

This battle saw the US 90th Infantry Division break the attack of Panzer Brigade 106 in and around the town of Mairy on 7-8 September 1944.  The conflict resulted in the near destruction of the German panzer brigade, severely reducing the capabilities of the German armored forces before the up-coming Lorraine counter-offensive.

As the German or American commander, can you do better?

Components

     An 11x17" map
     168 un-mounted, single-sided counters
     8 pages of rules and charts.

Corner Chess is a variant of chess for 2 or 4 players, on an 8x8 board with pieces of increased power (increased mobility for pawns and knight move for minor pieces), with piece starting in the corner.  In the base game a player wins game by capturing any enemy king.  In an advanced variant, players can win by getting their king across the board.

A collection of 4 games themed around fairy tales. It contains 4 games: 
1) Who awakens sleeping beauty with a kiss: The players must roll and move their way to sleeping beautys mouth. There are obstacles, which can only be removed by a rolling the exact number.
2) Bremen town musicians: This game is Ludo/Sorry/Pachisi/Mensch aergere Dich nicht.
3) Who finds the way to the witches house: A game with memory, players must match cards and place them on their path to the house.
4) Hare and tortoise: 1 or two tortoises (here hedgehogs) play against one hare by rolling dice. Both paths for hedgehogs and hare are the same length, but both hedgehogs can play together.

boardgame: September's Eagles: The Thompson Trophy Air Races, 1929-1939
description:
Along a course marked by pylons in front of a huge grandstand built especially for the event, “the Thompson” was the greatest aerial contest of its time. Pilots raced to their planes from a starting-gun's signal, flew off from a standing start, and then raced at speeds of hundreds of miles an hour, scores of feet above ground. The roar of the aircraft's engines were hardly drowned-out by that of the crowds as they witnessed aerial duels in which planes and pilots were pushed to their limits and sometimes beyond.

September's Eagles is a low level, fun to play game on the Thompson Trophy Air Race of 1929-1939. Players use cards to activate their planes and pilots, and to stymie (Trump) the moves of their opponents. Play is very interactive, intense and true to the history.

The colorful counters represent all of the major aircraft of the period, and pilot cards portray the famous aviators. In more detail, the game includes 36 different planes, from the first race winner (the Travelair R 'Mystery Ship') to the iconic Gee Bee R-1 and Roscoe Turner's 'Gilmore'. Planes are rated for endurance, turning radius, ability to climb or dive and speed. Thirty-three different pilots are represented, including pilots such as Jimmy Doolittle, Steve Wittman and Howard Hughes. Each pilot is rated for his skill level and fatigue. A deck of 54 Action Cards are used by players to move and maneuver their planes as well as rallying their planes and pilots. Players may also play cards on other players to disrupt their game.

Each game of September's Eagles comes with a full color and extensively illustrated Rules of Play booklet. In it is a comprehensive example of play to assist in learning the game. Along with the Rules of Play booklet comes a Racing Program which allows for the gaming of all the Thompson Trophy Races starting with the first in 1929 through to the final one in 1939.

boardgame: Blitzkrieg Undone: The Battle of Hannut
description:
Blitzkrieg Undone: The Battle of Hannut: May 12-13, 1940, is a low complexity level game of the battle between the Allied Armed forces and Germany's Wehrmacht on May 12th and 13th, 1940 at Hannut, Belgium (near Gembloux). Hannut was the largest tank battle to yet occur during the Second World War. The Germans launched Operation Yellow, the invasion of France and the Low Countries, on May 10, 1940. The Allies anticipated the Germans would strike through neutral Belgium as they had in WWI, and planned on confronting them there on the Belgian plain. The first large scale encounter occurred at Hannut, not far from the famous battlefields of Ramillies and Waterloo. Here the 1st and 2nd French Light Mechanized Divisions, heavily armed with tanks, ran into the 3rd and 4th Panzer Divisions.

The players, taking on the roles of the commander of the 1st French Army and German Army Group B during the Battle for Belgium.  The French, thinking that this was the main German effort, committed their most modern divisions to this battle.  For the Germans, this was a feint to tie down the French (and destroy their best units) while Army Group A raced through the Ardennes to the English Channel, to encircle the best Allied Armies. Tactically, the French were successful at Hannut.  Strategically, the Germans accomplished their goal of "soaking off" the French armor away from the main effort through the Ardennes, however the French 1st Army survived the campaign and was later instrumental in protecting the Dunkirk pocket, allowing hundreds of thousands of English and French troops to evacuate to England.

This is the sixth game in the Blitzkrieg in the West Series from High Flying Dice Games

Game Scale:
Game Turn: 1.5 hours
Hex: 1 miles / 1.61 kilometer
Units: Company

Game Inventory:
One 11x17" full color map (in 2 parts)
One single-side printed un-mounted countersheet (200 1/2" counters)(mounted counters are optional)
One 8-page rulebook
One Player aid Charts (TEC/CRT)

Solitaire Playability: Medium
Complexity Level: Low
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 1.5 to 2 hours
family: Series: Blitzkrieg in the West (Paul Rohrbaugh)

description:
The Blitzkrieg in the West series moves to the desert with this debut title.

Gazala was a resounding victory for the Desert Fox, Irwin Rommel, although at an extremely high cost in the DAK's tanks. Because of these losses, Rommel lacked the armored forces in subsequent battles to decisively defeat the Eighth Army as it retreated into Egypt.  This led directly to the end of the Afrika Korps pursuit, when they were directly defeated at the First battle of El Alamein.

Players will be challenged to do as well, or better, than their historical counterparts.  Fox's Gambit represents the first in a line of operational level games in High Flying Dice Games's new Blitzkrieg in North Africa series.

Abstract game. Played on a chessboard style grid, your pieces might attract or repel other pieces that you challenge. Feel the magnetic power!

Can you harness the magnets power to outwit your opponent? Feel the power of the magnets as each player fills the board with magnetic stones. You must use their force to your advantage so plot your move: decide where and how you'll play your next magnet. Place it onto the force field. Will you be save... or sorry?

The game contains 2,400 questions on the worlds favourite game as well as a die, score pad and pencil.
Each player takes it in turns to be the question master and they roll the die to see which of the six questions on the card they should ask. The additional twist in this game is that there are also picture cards included.

boardgame: The Gruffalo: Match and Memory Board Game
description:
Walk through the wood, looking at cards to see if you can remember where the animals are.
A simple match and memory board game featuring the characters from the hugely popular children's story The Gruffalo.
Discover what happens when the quick-thinking mouse comes face to face with an owl, a snake and a hungry fox.
This game has a strong educational component but is also great fun.

boardgame: The Gruffalo's Child: The Big Bad Mouse Board Game
description:
From the publisher:

The Gruffalo's Child is feeling brave so she tiptoes out of the gruffalo cave...Race around the board with the Gruffalo's child using colour recognition and counting. This Big Bad Mouse board game is based on the popular book series The Gruffalo's Child. Discover what happens when the Gruffalo's Child visits Snake, Owl, Fox and Mouse. The idea of the game is to make it back to the safety of the gruffalo cave, but beware, there is a Big Bad Mouse lurking in these woods who enjoys nothing more than a slice of Gruffalo pie! Spin the spinner and turn over the top card from the centre of the board that matches the colour on the spinner. If the card selected has footprints on it, move your Gruffalo's Child playing piece forward. The card may have 1, 2 or 3 footprints. This game has strong educational components and helps children learn through social interaction as well as taking turns and co-operation, all whilst having great fun.

boardgame: Inspector McClue: The Red Rose Murders
description:
Murder mystery dinner party game.
In the dog-eat-dog world of 1950s Hollywood there's only room for one top-dog - and he's the cigar-chomping agent to the stars, Rich Toad. So when he's found dead in suspicious circumstances, floating face-down in the swimming pool of his mansion, surrounded by a hundred scattered red roses, the whole film world is shocked to its core. There's no business like show business, they say, but when it comes to murder, a suspect is just a suspect. And one, or more, of Rick Toad's illustrious clients is about to get fingered for killing the most powerful man in entertainment. Your task is to discover who that killer is. Fortunately the famous Inspector McClue is on hand to assist your investigations.
The guests are: MARILYN MANSFIELD - The blonde bombshell whose flamboyant life both on-and-off-screen has delighted the gossip columnists for years.
ROCK HOUSTON - rugged, rough and rowdy, Houston was the biggest male star of them all, the ultimate in American masculinity; now he's going into politics and talking about running for the presidency.
MARLON MEAN - The most famous teenager in the world. Mean is actually 27-years-old now, but he's still making a career out of screen rebellion.
PRINCESS KELLY OF RURITANIA - before her marriage to the Crown Prince, the most eligible bachelor in Europe, Princess Kelly was simple Kelly Kennedy, the sophisticated star of romantic comedies.
MADAME VELDA - clairvoyant to the stars, she has been a familiar figure in Hollywood circles for many years, predicting the future with surprising accuracy.
WAYNE FONDA - also known as the Hillbilly Hero, Fonda is a rock and roll singer who might just cut it in the movies.
MAE VEST - author, actress and activist, Miss Vest is seldom seen without a devoted young man on her arm, a glass of champagne in her hand, and a quip on her lips.
SALVADOR DILLY-DALLY - Europe's most famous surrealist artist has come to Hollywood to work in the movies, and to make his fortune.
The game includes invitations: simply choose which of your friends play which devious suspect and invite them to come along as that character. Also included are menu suggestions, recipes and of course, a really tasty murder to solve!

Children's trivia in a tin box.
Three hundred colourful cards with trivia questions, fascinating facts and actions. These fascinating questions and facts about the subject in question also contain actions to perform.
Decide your own objective to win, first to get 5 questions right wins, that sort of thing.

Children must be quick to play this grabbing game!

The plastic board has a central slot into which a green plastic thingie (for want of a better word) is inserted. When the thingie is removed, up pop random Meerkats and you have to grab one if you can.

Each player must try and prevent losing their meerkat to the dreaded cobra. Position your meerkat, start the spinner and get ready to grab your meerkat before the cobra strikes.

Whoever has the most meerkat's at the end, wins the game!

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Khazad-dûm
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The first deluxe expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, Khazad-dûm transports you to the ancient realm of the Dwarves with 165 new cards (with three copies each of its player cards), including new scenarios, encounter sets, heroes, allies, attachments, and events.

From Orcs and Trolls to Ringwraiths and Dragons, Middle-earth is a rich and expansive world, filled with fantastic and iconic creatures. As you venture into the ancient Dwarven realm of Khazad-dûm, you'll enter a place in Middle-earth far removed from the settings and creatures represented by the Core Set encounter cards. Dark tunnels teem with goblins, and any wrong turn could send you plummeting through pits to find yourself confronted by older and fouler things than Orcs.

Therefore, in addition to expanding your existing game experience, Khazad-dûm introduces nine all-new encounter sets. These unique sets, when combined into encounter decks (as instructed by the rules), make up the three included scenarios in Khazad-dûm.

Note: To play the scenarios in the subsequent "Dwarrowdelf Cycle" of Adventure Packs, you will need the new encounter sets in this expansion.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Matriarchy Troop Pack for Tannhäuser further expands the Matriarchy faction by adding two stunningly painted miniatures – the fearless Boepripacy Soldat and the flamethrower-wielding Zazigag Soldat – along with game tokens, equipments cards, and a detailed 12-page rulebook with an all-new scenario.

This scenario pits a Nippon Accords force against an alliance of UMTOMA soldiers, as the Reich's Karl Zermann attempts to recover an ancient artifact for Von Heïzinger.

(note: this "upgrade pack" requires the base Puzzle Strike game to play)

Upgrade your puzzle striking with several new components to enhance aesthetics, immersion and accessibility as well as new puzzle chips and rebalanced character chips.

This upgrade pack includes:

    4 identical playmats
    4 unique screens to hide your chips without holding them in your hand
    3 new puzzle chips x 5 copies each

    A rebalanced set of the 10 characters in the base set (30 character chips)


The playmats help you separate your gem pile and other game zones, plus they look great! They're made of the same high quality mouse pad material as the Yomi playmats, and you could even use them as mouse pads.

This upgrade pack is compatible with the second edition (pink boxed) version of Puzzle Strike, and not the first edition Deluxe with the wooden chips.

description:
User summary:
A tile-laying game for 2–4 players. Players have their starting point in one of the corners of a thought 7x7 tile map, with the goal in the middle. Taking turns of laying down tiles with straight corridors, bends, T-crossings and so on, some of which are one-way, the player who first connects his starting point with the goal wins the game. Among the special tiles are tiles that allow you to turn, move or remove an already laid tile.

In the abandoned and uncharted spaces of the solar system a small group of daredevil pilots fly their spacecraft through dangerous race courses known as Circuits. Set up in advance by a shadowy group of scouts and bookies, each Circuit is unique. Some wind through planetary canyons, others through old mining tunnels bored through asteroids or through the swirling rocks of planetary rings. Racers must dodge obstacles as well as the weapons fire of their opponents to cross the finish line first to win fame and fortune!

Solar Circuit Racing is a sci-fi racing game for 2-6 players. The game is played on a modular, customizable course made out of map tiles. Moving into a tile with an obstacle requires a piloting roll. Failing the piloting roll results in something bad happening, like smashing into an asteroid or scraping a wing along a canyon wall. Damage reduces the capabilities of your ship. Once you pass the roll, you can advance into the space and move on.

Each turn, you will have to manage your limited engine power, and make decisions about how to spend it. Engine power can be used to advance through the course, to evade enemy weapons, and to make maneuvering through obstacles easier. Spend too much power on maneuvering and your opponents will jump into the lead, too much on advancing and you'll splatter against the obstacles. The first player to complete the decided on number of laps wins.

Quorsum is an abstract, strategic racing game for two players.  The goal is simple enough: move your tokens to the opposite corner of the 4x4 board.  Quorsum is unique, however, in that the board is never the same from game to game; rather, the 16 board tiles are picked at random before the game, so that players must constantly react to an unexpected setup.  Not only that, but the board continues to change throughout the game, as players try to flip key tiles from light to dark or vice versa.

Luck is minimal: even the dice require skill, since players may decide how many of their dice to use for each important move.  The result is a fast-paced game in which players must constantly calculate the best path to take and the best way to hinder the opponent's tokens.  Although the experienced player will usually win, still there is often a chance for both players right up until the last move.

Quorsum avoids both the predictable setup of most strategy games, and the swings of luck in most dice games.  The players' fate, therefore, is always in their own hands.

boardgame: Postman Pat: A Very Special Delivery Board Game
description:
A simple roll and move game for the very young.

Another busy day for Postman Pat as he dashes around collecting Special Delivery parcels for the residents of Greendale. Be the first to find and collect the most matching parcels. Players spin the spinner to move around the board, depending on the number of parcels pictured on the square they landed on they get to turn over the same number of tiles in the middle of the board, if they get a matching pair they get to keep them. If they land on one of the character squares they get to jumble some of the tiles.

The publisher says this is a memory game with a jumble shuffle twist (whatever that is)!
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

boardgame: Postman Pat: Mayhem at the Sorting Office Board Game

boardgame: Scooby-Doo! and the Monster Mall Board Game
description:
Game based on the characters from the animated TV show.

The publisher says;
Scooby doo and the gang have made a quick stop at the late night mall to do some last minute shopping.
Although, unknown to them, they aren't the only customers there!

Wandering around the aisles are three scary monsters who intend to make their shopping experience one they will never forget! So grab a trolley, head to the aisles, and lets do some shopping!

This is a 2 player deck building game that has player interaction, balance, and strategy as the primary focuses. Every game turn has both players taking actions. We have defense and offense represented, so you are not just at the mercy of the cards. You do not have to add to your deck each turn, so the size of your deck is up to you, and there for you are in control of how likely you are to draw the cards you need.

A simple word game. Each player rolls the eight dice. Seven of the dice are white with a letter on each face. The eighth die is black and has a symbol on it such as a question mark. Just make a word that goes with the symbol.
The publisher describes it as a cross between Scrabble and Boggle.
The game comes in a carry case so it makes a good game to take on holiday.

boardgame: The Enigma of Leonardo: Quintis Fontis
description:
The Enigma of Leonardo: Quintis Fontis is a specialized version of "The Enigma of Leonardo" designed for hardcore players, for those, that considered the core version too simple. Designed for those players, that enjoy strategic planning and quick thinking.

QF is played using the same cards as the core set – each card has two symbols on it, there are 12 different symbols altogether. Most of the cards are identical to the ones in the core set, while others have different symbol combinations on them. The set also includes a few special text cards (action cards).

The goal of the game is the same as in The Enigma of Leonardo, to collect Secret Keys, by making sequences of identical symbols but this time the game is played on one big field. The field itself is made during game play, the only restriction being its size – 5x5 cards. The first card is placed in the middle of the playing field, players then take turns at playing cards, increasing the field. In the end, the first card that was played might not even be in the middle anymore, it could be on the edge.

Players can not only play cards on empty spaces, they can play cards on top of each other and can even replace cards from their hands with the ones on the playing field. The only condition is that the cards being replaced must share a common symbol. This playing mechanic allows for more complex combos, by careful planning, one can gradually replace all the cards on the field with symbols that the player needs.

Just like in The Enigma of Leonardo, in QF players receive Secret Keys when they successfully make a row of cards with the same symbol. But now you can get symbols not only for 3 cards in a row, but for four and even five identical symbols. Qunitis fontis actually means – “the fifth symbol”, when translated from Latin. Rows can be made horizontally, diagonally and vertically just like in the core game but now you can receive the same key more than once by first making a row of three symbols and then adding cards with the fourth and fifth symbol.

But it sounds much easier than it actually is. The game now uses a special counter-marker called “veto”. After playing a card, the player may choose to move the marker on any card (even the one he just played) on the playing field. The next player cannot play any cards that share a symbol with the marked card. It’s up to the players to decide should they move the marker each turn or keep it in one place blocking a certain symbol combination for the entire game.

Another new addition to QF are the action cards. There aren’t much compared to another expansion of the game The Enigma of Leonardo – Novem – but they can alter the flow of the game. With these cards you can steal symbols from your opponents and prevent them from gaining new ones.

The player who successfully collects seven Keys is the winner. If the deck is used up and no one has collected seven Keys, the one to collect the most is the winner. In case of a tie a key gradation has been provided.

QF is basically a harder version of The Enigma of Leonardo: the rules are different but the cards used are practically identical. Meaning you can try playing The Enigma of Leonardo using QF or vice versa. It will be a slightly different game, but it’s still possible.


Reimplements:

    The Enigma of Leonardo

description:
Most of the cards in this card game are letter cards, with which a word is formed; the rest are special action cards. Each player has six cards at the start of the game; the goal is to get rid of one's cards first.
The first player plays a card on the table; this card will be the first letter of a word that s/he thinks about (but doesn't say out loud). The next player adds a second letter to the word, etc. There are many jokers, so there are also many possibilities for what a word might be. When a player doubts that the last player can still think of a word starting with the letters on the table, s/he can call the bluff. If the previous players cannot produce a word, the bluffing player must pick up the cards from the table, if s/he can, the accusing player picks them up. Instead of playing a card, a player can also choose to draw a new card from the stack, but still takes responsibility for the word on the table (i.e., if called, must produce a word with these letters).
By placing a facedown card on the table, any player (out of turn order), can announce that a word is complete. The letters are then discarded and a new word is started.

There are various special cards with which players can be forced to say a word with the current letters, cards can be passed to other players, etc.

When a player plays his or her last card, the other players count the points printed on their remaining cards. These are their negative scores.

Aimed at children, this is a game reminiscent of Taboo, where you must describe an object on the card but without using certain key words. For instance, it might say 'Balloon' and you cannot use the words 'pop’, ‘inflate’, ‘bang’ or ‘float’?

The subjects are graded so that different age groups can play.

The cards are dispensed from a yellow plastic head and the cards becomes its tongue, so to speak.

Manga Party comes with two copies of 26 cards (one with red back, one with beige) that each feature a scene drawn manga-style; it's up to the players to bring the party!

To set up the game, the 26 beige cards are laid face-up on the table. Three red-backed "mime" cards are shuffled with the 26 red-backed scene cards to form a deck.

On a turn, the active player draws the top card from the deck, then acts out a scene, complete with whatever thoughts, cries, declarations, etc. best fit the word bubble or thought bubble on the card. The other players race to identify which beige card matches the scene being acted out. Each player can choose only one card, and no card can be chosen more than once. If the correct card is chosen, the guesser scores the beige card and the actor keeps the red card; otherwise both scene cards are removed from the game and the actor takes the "Malus" card (whether from the table or another player).

If a player draws a Mime card, he discards it, draws the next card, and acts out the scene but without any dialogue or sounds.

The game ends when either two beige cards remain on the table or after the third Mime card has been resolved. Players scores one point per card collected, with the holder of the Malus card losing one point. The player with the most points wins.

Object: Be the first to get eight stamps on your score sheet.

Each player gets 4 cards (one for each pony) and a score sheet. Before each turn, every player chooses one card to place face-down. The player whose turn it is then spins the merry-go-round, and when it stops, one of four colors will display through the merry-go-round windows. 
   Anybody whose face-down card matches the color displaying through the windows may use the pony of that color to stamp his/her score sheet.

Contents:

Mechanical merry-go-round with ball top
Sheet of stickers for merry-go-round base
4 stamper pony pieces with stamp caps (Posey, Heart Throb, Cherries Jubilee, & Medley)
16 pony cards (4 of each pony)
1 pad of score sheets to stamp and color
Instruction sheet

Cataclysm is a large board chess variant (12 X 16)

It uses most of the basic chess pieces from FIDE chess, plus a range of additional, mostly short range pieces.

New pieces include;

Grand Rook: The Grand Rook can move as either a standard Rook, or as a Cannon from Chinese Chess (that is, it can capture an enemy piece by leaping over exactly one piece of either color and capturing the first piece beyond, if that piece is an enemy piece.)

Sorceress: The Sorceress is a 2-space area-mover. She may make either a single step in any direction as a King, or may make two consecutive steps in any direction and may change directions in between. The only limitations are that the Sorceress may not return to her original square (thereby making a null-move) and may not capture a piece on the first move and continue on - her move ends with any capture.

The Elephant may slide up to four spaces diagonally, or may step a single space horizontally or vertically. This piece has no relation to the Elephant of Shatranj.

The Tiger may slide up to four spaces horizontally or vertically, or may step a single space diagonally.

The Duke may slide up to three spaces in any direction. He moves like a Queen in Chess, except that he is limited to a range of only three squares

and others

Each player has 40 pieces (including 16 pawns)

boardgame: Lost Worlds: Dino Fight Series – Allosaur
description:
A spin-off from the popular Lost World Combat Book series. Instead of fantasy heroes, the players take the roles of fighting Dinosaurs.

Each player neeeds a booklet to play. Booklets are traded and, as each player selects a move, a reference grid determines the result (wounds loss, off balance maneuvers, and so on). Combat continues until one Dinosaur or the other expires.
family: Series: Lost Worlds Dino Fight Series (Greysea Games)

description:
This expansion is made of 10 (36 cards total) of the building cards BGGers suggested in this GeekList, and the ones that got most votes went to the Finalist Round.

Keeping in mind the results of the round, the Cambridge Games Factory employees chose what cards to keep. They were then helped by some BGGers in playtesting/tweaking them and the final results are here.

This Promo expansion was reserved to the Kickstarters supporters of the Black Box Edition, but it's not excluded that there will be wider print runs.
The expansion was printed in two versions, one with Ed Carter's cartooney artwork and the other using Heiko Günther's essential style.

One note on compatibility: The expansion cards created with the original artwork are 63 mm x 88 mm.  The 2007 printing of GtR I.V had cards that were 64.5 mm x 89.5 mm.  If you sleeve your cards or have a later printing (which is the most common edition of I.V), then the promo cards will work fine.  If not, you will find that the size of the cards is slightly mismatched.

description:
Electronic game which Waddingtons probably saw as a competitor to take on Milton Bradley's Simon.

It plays 4 different games - Match Me, Break Out, Hot Corners and Music Maker. The main game was Match Me which was nearly an exact copy of the Simon game. Each game had 11 different ways to play them. The game has sound and moving lights.

Trivial Pursuit Bite Size ... Our favourite game now doesn't take a day to play! You can ask and answer 600 questions about ingredients, fast food, techniques and utensils, fine dining, drinks and world food.

This is another in the "Bite-Size" range of Trivial Pursuit games. It is intended as a portable travel game. The game is packaged in a wedge ("piece-of-pie") shaped box.

The game does not feature a game board, and thus, there is no roll-and-move mechanic. Instead, players roll the color-coded die, and answers a question matching the color rolled. If a player correctly answers a question, he may "go for a wedge" by answering another question in the same category. If he does so, he keeps that card. If the player answers a question incorrectly, it is the next player's turn. Play ends when a player has collected six cards/correctly answered 12 questions.

For 2 or more players.

From publisher:

Miss Russian Night (How many women can you seduce in one night?) is a card board game that has been produced under license with the erotic Russian Night TV channel. The players are the guests at a night club who are flirting with 8 different girls. The winner is the player who is able to successfully lure the most women to bed.

Each girl is a real model from the TV channel. They are represented by ten different cards, all varying in their level of intimacy from the proper “one” whom a player can eventually get, to the absolutely naked “ten”. The playing field has three different zones: a stack of cards at the dance floor, the bar table with four open cards and the strip podium. The winner of the game will be the one who can control his desires and use his skills

From publisher:

The card game duel The Fighter (Men can do anything!) has been created based on the popular Russian reality-show about mixed martial arts: M1-Fighter. Now, anyone can step into the ring and participate in fights with their TV heroes! Swings and uppercuts, throws and low-kicks, holds and yellow cards – this original fight simulator contains all those elements which we love to see in the martial arts. The game presents eight fighters: actual participants from the TV show. They all have unique fighting abilities: strength, speed, stamina and coordination. Using their skills, the fighters attack each other, avoid hits and maneuver in an attempt to gain a tactical advantage.

The unique game mechanics lie in the interaction of the move cards and 40 multicolored dice. However, to win, you will need not only luck, but also the ability to plan your actions and turn the game situation in your favor.

Another failed attempt by Waddingtons, to get into the electronic board game market.

The pitch has several holes in it at strategic points. If the ball rolls into one of your holes, you press a button behind the goal and the ball is 'pinged' out of the hole and into another hole. You hope to keep possession and eventually shoot into the opponent's goal.

It had sound effects and an electronic score board but, as a game, it is almost entirely devoid of skill. You just press buttons.

Juvenile horse-riding themed dice-rolling game in two phases.

In part one, "Schooling Track", players throw a d6 to move around and collect (or lose) horseshoes.

In part two, "Cross Country Track", players use a die numbered 1,2,3 to move and attempt jumps. Horseshoe cards can be used to improve a die roll.

It is one of the Milton Bradley "Flipsiders" games that resembles an audio cassette until you "flip" the game open.

The archaeologist takes the green marker and begins at the entrance to the pyramid.
The other player, the mummy, begins at the bottom of the pyramid.
A ruby is set in another room at the bottom of the pyramid.

The archaeologist will get the ruby and back out of the pyramid.
The mummy has to kill the archaeologist.

Mod'Collection is one of the Milton Bradley "Flipsiders" games that resembles an audio cassette until you "flip" the game open.

Each player will get a full set of clothes (shoes, skirt, top and jacket) to buy in shops. After that, they need to reach the final case.

Each player take pictures of wild animals.

Each player moves around on a course of 6 animals and have the ability to take pictures.

The winner is the player who made the best photos (15 points photos).

A player takes the trafficker who should retrieve four items before fleeing by helicopter.
The other will pursue the trafficker in the city.

The two players move in the city with the dials. For the prosecuter, it must stop exactly on the case of the trafficker. The trafficker will go to the boxes objects in a specific order before running to the heliport.

It is a strategic game in which opponents should make them conducive chance.
You can play two, three, four and two teams of two players.

Each player receives 15 coins to start the game.
The first action of the game is to fill the board with so many games.
After each set the players traded currencies as the play tells them. And redo the box
score play.
The game ends when one player gets all the coins.
Each player is identified with one of the letters: A, B, C or D, and with a color: Blue,
Red, Green and Yellow.


Es un juego estratégico, en el que los adversarios deben hacer que el azar les sea
propicio.
Pueden jugar dos, tres, cuatro y dos equipos de dos jugadores.

Cada jugador recibe 15 monedas al iniciar la partida.
La primera acción del juego es llenar el tablero con los tantos de juego.
Después en cada lance los jugadores intercambian las monedas que la jugada les indica.
Y rehacen la puntuación de la casilla jugada.
El juego finaliza cuando un jugador se queda con todas las monedas.
Cada jugador se identifica con una de las letras: A, B, C o D; y con un color: Azul,
Rojo, Verde y Amarillo.

It's time to break out and hit the dance floor! You can boogie to one of the three beats on the electronic dance unit or play your own slammin' tunes to groove to. The more you play, the better you'll get...that's when you start improvising! The dancer that scores the most points, by collecting cards while dancing, become the Bratz Disco Queen.

Contents:

     electronic dance unit-  pink & white speaker, 
     gameboard, 
     pack of 36 double- sided dance cards, 
     instructions

Canada Quiz is a trivia that integrates tactical elements.

The goal of the game is to be the first player (or team) to get the Canada flag by answering properly the questions.

The board has four playing track (one per player or team), leading to the flag in the centre of the board.  Each player (or team) has four markers.  The dice is rolled to bring a marker in play and or to move it along the track;  however, a marker can be moved only if the question asked is properly answered.  When a player answers without a "hint" (without asking which are the multiple choices listed on the card), that player can have an other question, so an other opportunity to move forward.  Under some conditions, the questions may also be used to perform a "power-play", which could force an other player to move backward.  The first player to reach the flag and to answer properly the question wins the game.

Canada Quiz has 250 question cards, with four questions (with multiple answers) on each, for a total of 1000 questions.  The trivia game is based on early the history of Canada, mainly covering the historical period from the very beginnings up to the Confederation.  Canada Quiz has been published in English and in French.

44 pages.  Includes 10 scenarios, an APC player aid chart and a large (DASL) map, BF2.

8 of the scenarios are East front, with one west front scenario, and one end war Russian v. Japanese scenario.

The scenarios are:

RBF-9: Rolling Stones

RBF-10: Tip Off at Tauroggen

RBF-11: Gut Check

RBF-12: Sverdlikova Melee

RBF-13: Neubauer Battalion

RBF-14: Kampfgruppe Lang

RBF-15: Shumilinmo

RBF-16: The Bitter End

RBF-17: Third Hotspot

RBF-18: First Day at Fuchin

Pants! is a super silly family card game of trouser-collecting and wardrobe-stealing. Build your wardrobe of classy pants, sporty pants, adventure pants, or casual pants before your opponents do.

But hold tight to your trousers because they can get stolen right from under your nose!

Each card is artfully designed with a retro-funky pants illustration. Let’s play!

The cards consist of different wardrobe categories: Classy, Sporty, Casual, Adventure. There are four unique types of pants in each wardrobe category. For example, Classy Pants consists of stripey dress pants, tuxedo pants, a gown, and a kilt.

The object is to collect two full sets of pants from two different categories. The first player to do that wins. There are wild cards you can play to thwart your opponents' pants collection: Suspenders and gift card. Just when you're ready to lay down your winning set of pants, another player can steal your set or make you put it back. Ouch. 
There's also an underwear wild card you can use with three pairs of pants in a category to make a complete set. Wee!

Game play goes like this: Each player is dealt five cards. In a turn of play, you can:
• Lay down a complete set of pants 
• Discard pants cards you don't need 
• Pick from the discard pile to get cards you need

Its Kickstarter campaign failed to reach its funding goal on October 7 2011.

Hansa: The Compass is a small expansion for Michael Schacht's board game Hansa that randomizes the direction of movement on the game board.

Before the game begins, one player spins the "compass" tile. Depending on which side is face-up, the merchant ship either moves with or against the arrows on the game board for the entirety of the game.

Early 20th century action game.

Your yacht is attached to a length of string in such a way that if the string is pulled, raised or shaken, the player can make the yacht 'sail' along the string to the finish line. The strings are attached to the table. The box illustrates rather well the set-up and the game-play.

Really cute cats are in the illustration on the box, watching mother and son playing the game.

One of Waddingtons' Target Series games, aimed at the customer on a strict budget.

Each player has a plastic 'racket' that is rather like a see-saw. You put the 'ball' onto your racket, push the handle of the racket down and, hopefully, the ball would land within the confines of the board. If successful, your opponent now returns the ball in the same way.

Both tennis and badminton play in the same way and you score as in the real rules for either sport.

Similar to Table Badminton

boardgame: The Game of Punch and Judy
description:
Simple race game. The object is to reach the end of the track first so that you can see the Punch & Judy show.
The track has several negative squares, Lose 1 turn, lose 2 turns and Go Back to Start. There are no positive squares.
You need an exact throw to win.

Numenko-in-a-bag features game play and components similar to the Numenko board game - but without a game board and with a few other changes.

The wooden tiles feature digits (0-9), a Multichoice symbol (that stands for the player's choice of addition, subtraction, multiplication, division or "is equal to"), and a Freechoice symbol (that can be a digit or Multichoice). Each player starts with 20 randomly drawn tiles (if playing with 2-4 players; 15 tiles otherwise) and one Freechoice tile. The remaining tiles are mixed face-down on the table.

Playing at the same time, players reveal their tiles and try to create valid equations crossword-style in their own playing area. A Multichoice or Freesymbol tile can be used as a different operator or number in intersecting equations. At any time, a player can return an unwanted tile to the stock in exchange for two newly drawn tiles. If a player uses all of his tiles in one or more equations, then he declares "Done" and draws four new tiles from the stock. Players can rearrange tiles any number of times to incorporate new tiles.

The game ends if a player wants to swap when fewer than two tiles remain in reserve or declares "Done" with fewer than four tiles in reserve. The player who has used all tiles in equations when the game ends wins. If no player fits this description, then players sum their tiles in hand (with a Freechoice tile worth ten points), and the player with the lowest sum wins.

This game is a chess variant, -according to wikipedia definitions and chessvariants.org- with fantastic theme. Each player controls an army. There are four different armies to choose and each one has their own particular attributes. In every army there are 4 soldiers, 2 sergeants, 2 lieutenants, 2 generals, one special unit and a King.
This game is played on a hex board.
Each piece has some symbols that indicate their moving and capturing capacities and, sometimes, a special ability.

In 19th Century Britain, sports were invented and developed to provide the idle rich with a means of betting large sums of money against each other. In The Victorian Gamer you are one of the idle rich.
No aristocrat worthy of his name would have been without a fast running footman or a stable boy who was a particularly able jockey. He would put up his ‘man’ against another noble’s ‘man’ for an agreed sum. The sport was incidental; it was the outcome that counted, with the winner taking all. And of course, a whole new industry of side betting with bookies quickly emerged.
The Victorian Gamer mirrors this era. There are three different events – running, rowing and horseracing - each one having its own unique gameplay. In addition, players can make bets against each other and/or with the bookie. And furthermore, to add a bit of extra spice there are three differently numbered dice to form the odds and affect the outcomes. 
In short, there are three different races, two ways of betting, but only one winner. For friends and  family with even the tiniest element of a competitive streak, The Victorian Gamer will provide hours of fun, not to mention a few squabbles, huffs  and sulks. Warning – NOT A GAME FOR BAD LOSERS.

The Know It Game is the trivia game where simply answering correctly isn’t enough. To win you have to successfully bluff your opponents into believing you know the answer.
The questions are original, amusing and informative and the games can be traded, swapped or collected. 
Each box consists of two broad themes and there are over 200 questions per theme split into five games.
Answer correctly, you win the card. Claim to ‘Know It’, you could win two.

Delicious cakes are lined up for you! Just eat as many as you can and as fast as you can!

In this game, there are four types of cakes, and they may fit into the four empty slots one has in front of oneself. Each player has a deck of cards and starts drawing cards and placing them out on the slots, simultanously. The new cards on hand are placed on top of the corresponding piles. In addition to different cake types, the cards also display between 1-3 cakes/card. It is therefore better to place 1-cake cards on other players' piles/slots and the 3-cake cards on your own piles.

The game ends when the first player to finish his/her deck shouts "I'm full!". That player gets an additional 3 bonus VPs. Each player then counts the number of cakes, getting 1VP/cake and adding them with any bonus VPs. The winner is the one who gains the most VPs (i.e. the one who has eaten the most cakes in the shortest time).


The new version of My Cake! has been released in September 2011. In this second version there are 108 cards and the brand new artwork makes these cakes even more delicious! The addition of two new rules of play offers more variety in your gaming experience.

In Penguin Panic: The Great Icescape, the players control penguin placement in a prison yard. Using a Venn diagram the players place penguins into various places that will benefit themselves, filling up the area they have control of. Each player needs to help penguins escape from the prison. Careful placement allows the penguins to escape the region they have filled up (without helping their opponents too much, due to overlapping the Venn diagram prison yard allows).

Object of the game is try Your skill with words to win the greatest number of crossword cards.

In his turn player throws the 6 dice and attempts to form a word(s). If the player can use all six letters then he takes the top card of the card pile but he can only keep it if he can join it to his dice arrangement by forming another word or extending the excisting word.

When all the cards have been claimed the player with the most cards is the winner.

Object of the game is to throw a series of dice combinations to win.

In his turn player throw 5 dice and one of the combination, as shown on the cards appears. He may choose it or try a higher combination re-throwing some of his dice. If he fails he cannot then claim the original combination he abandoned.
If he was successful the next player must attempt the combination re-throwing his dice up to three time.

Crucible of Steel is a scenario pack that focuses solely on the southern shoulder of Operation Citadel. It definitely captured the feel of Kursk, and the scenarios have a wide variety of actions. There are mid to larger scenarios, and there are also numerous tournament- sized scenarios.

32 scenarios
3 New Double-Wide map boards
3 New Standard map boards
2 NEW countersheets for Kursk
4 updated countersheets from Blood and Jungle
1/2 updated countersheet from Operation Cobra
Magazine with detailed articles
Vehicle and Ordnance Notes
Rules pages
